1	-- Hiive Deal Pipeline Monitor — Domain Schema
2	-- Seven entity types; agent checkpoint state lives in agent_checkpoints.db (separate).
3	-- All timestamps are ISO8601 strings (UTC).
4	
5	PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
6	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
7	
8	-- ── Issuers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issuers (
10	    issuer_id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
11	    name                 TEXT NOT NULL,
12	    typical_response_days INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 5,
13	    rofr_window_days     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 30,
14	    multi_layer_rofr     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- BOOL
15	    sector               TEXT,
16	    created_at           TEXT NOT NULL
17	);
18	
19	-- ── Parties ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS parties (
21	    party_id             TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
22	    party_type           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (party_type IN ('buyer', 'seller', 'counsel', 'internal')),
23	    display_name         TEXT NOT NULL,
24	    is_first_time        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- BOOL
25	    prior_breakage_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
26	    created_at           TEXT NOT NULL
27	);
28	
29	-- ── Deals ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS deals (
31	    deal_id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
32	    issuer_id            TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issuers(issuer_id),
33	    buyer_id             TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES parties(party_id),
34	    seller_id            TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES parties(party_id),
35	    shares               INTEGER NOT NULL,
36	    price_per_share      REAL NOT NULL,
37	    stage                TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (stage IN (
38	        'bid_accepted', 'docs_pending', 'issuer_notified', 'rofr_pending',
39	        'rofr_cleared', 'signing', 'funding', 'settled', 'broken'
40	    )),
41	    stage_entered_at     TEXT NOT NULL,  -- simulated-time ISO8601
42	    rofr_deadline        TEXT,           -- simulated-time ISO8601 or NULL
43	    responsible_party    TEXT CHECK (responsible_party IN ('buyer', 'seller', 'issuer', 'hiive_ts', NULL)),
44	    blockers             TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',   -- JSON array of {kind, description, since}
45	    risk_factors         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',   -- JSON {is_first_time_buyer, prior_breakage_count, ...}
46	    created_at           TEXT NOT NULL,
47	    updated_at           TEXT NOT NULL
48	);
49	
50	-- ── Events (append-only) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
51	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
52	    event_id             TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
53	    deal_id              TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES deals(deal_id),
54	    event_type           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (event_type IN (
55	        'stage_transition', 'doc_received', 'doc_requested',
56	        'comm_outbound', 'comm_inbound', 'comm_sent_agent_recommended'
57	    )),
58	    occurred_at          TEXT NOT NULL,  -- simulated-time ISO8601 (FR-019)
59	    created_at           TEXT NOT NULL,  -- real-time ISO8601 (FR-019)
60	    summary              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
61	    payload              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'  -- JSON freeform
62	);
63	
64	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_deal_id ON events(deal_id);
65	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);
66	
67	-- ── Ticks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	-- Load-bearing for FR-024 idempotency: written atomically at tick start.
69	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ticks (
70	    tick_id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
71	    mode                 TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (mode IN ('real_time', 'simulated')),
72	    tick_started_at      TEXT NOT NULL,   -- real-time ISO8601
73	    tick_completed_at    TEXT,            -- NULL until complete
74	    deals_screened       INTEGER,

76	    errors               TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'  -- JSON array of error strings
77	);
78	
79	-- ── Agent Observations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	-- One row per (tick_id, deal_id). Dedup via UNIQUE constraint (FR-024).
81	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agent_observations (
82	    observation_id       TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
83	    tick_id              TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES ticks(tick_id),
84	    deal_id              TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES deals(deal_id),
85	    observed_at          TEXT NOT NULL,
86	    severity             TEXT CHECK (severity IN ('informational', 'watch', 'act', 'escalate', NULL)),
87	    dimensions_evaluated TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',  -- JSON list of RiskDimension names
88	    reasoning            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',  -- JSON: full structured reasoning incl. enrichment_chain
89	    intervention_id      TEXT REFERENCES interventions(intervention_id),  -- linked if drafted
90	    UNIQUE (tick_id, deal_id)  -- idempotency anchor (FR-024)

92	
93	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_observations_deal_id ON agent_observations(deal_id);
94	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_observations_tick_id ON agent_observations(tick_id);
95	
96	-- ── Interventions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
97	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS interventions (

100	    observation_id       TEXT REFERENCES agent_observations(observation_id),
101	    intervention_type    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (intervention_type IN (
102	        'outbound_nudge', 'internal_escalation', 'brief_entry'
103	    )),
104	    recipient_type       TEXT CHECK (recipient_type IN ('buyer', 'seller', 'issuer', 'internal', NULL)),
105	    draft_subject        TEXT,
106	    draft_body           TEXT NOT NULL,
107	    final_text           TEXT,   -- NULL until approved/edited
108	    status               TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN (
109	        'pending', 'approved', 'edited', 'dismissed'
110	    )),
111	    reasoning_ref        TEXT,   -- observation_id whose reasoning produced this
112	    created_at           TEXT NOT NULL,
113	    approved_at          TEXT,
114	    dismissed_at         TEXT
115	);
116	
117	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_interventions_deal_id ON interventions(deal_id);
118	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_interventions_status ON interventions(status);

1	"""
2	Data-access helpers for the domain database.
3	All writes use INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency (FR-024).
4	"""
5	
6	from __future__ import annotations
7	
8	import json
9	import sqlite3
10	import uuid
11	from datetime import datetime
12	from typing import Any
13	
14	from hiive_monitor import clock as clk
15	
16	
17	# ── Deals ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18	
19	
20	def get_live_deals(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> list[dict]:
21	    """Return all deals not in settled or broken stage."""
22	    rows = conn.execute(
23	        "SELECT * FROM deals WHERE stage NOT IN ('settled', 'broken') ORDER BY stage_entered_at"
24	    ).fetchall()
25	    return [dict(r) for r in rows]
26	
27	
28	def get_deal(conn: sqlite3.Connection, deal_id: str) -> dict | None:
29	    row = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM deals WHERE deal_id = ?", (deal_id,)).fetchone()
30	    return dict(row) if row else None
31	
32	
33	def get_all_deals(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> list[dict]:
34	    rows = conn.execute("SELECT * FROM deals ORDER BY created_at").fetchall()
35	    return [dict(r) for r in rows]
36	
37	
38	# ── Events ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39	
40	
41	def get_events(conn: sqlite3.Connection, deal_id: str, limit: int | None = None) -> list[dict]:
42	    if limit:
43	        rows = conn.execute(
44	            "SELECT * FROM events WHERE deal_id = ? ORDER BY occurred_at DESC LIMIT ?",
45	            (deal_id, limit),
46	        ).fetchall()
47	        return list(reversed([dict(r) for r in rows]))
48	    rows = conn.execute(
49	        "SELECT * FROM events WHERE deal_id = ? ORDER BY occurred_at", (deal_id,)
50	    ).fetchall()
51	    return [dict(r) for r in rows]
52	
53	
54	def insert_event(
55	    conn: sqlite3.Connection,
56	    deal_id: str,
57	    event_type: str,
58	    occurred_at: datetime,
59	    summary: str = "",
60	    payload: dict | None = None,
61	    event_id: str | None = None,
62	) -> str:
63	    eid = event_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
64	    real_now = clk.now().isoformat()
65	    conn.execute(
66	        """INSERT OR IGNORE INTO events
67	           (event_id, deal_id, event_type, occurred_at, created_at, summary, payload)
68	           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
69	        (eid, deal_id, event_type, occurred_at.isoformat(), real_now, summary, json.dumps(payload or {})),
70	    )
71	    return eid
72	
73	
74	# ── Ticks ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	
77	def start_tick(conn: sqlite3.Connection, mode: str, tick_id: str | None = None) -> str:

1	"""
2	LLM client — structured tool-use calls via OpenRouter (OpenAI-compatible API).
3	
4	All calls go through call_structured(), which:
5	  - Forces tool-use output via Pydantic model_json_schema()
6	  - Retries transient errors with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts)
7	  - Retries parse failures once with a corrective reprompt (FR-025)
8	  - Caches by (tick_id, deal_id, call_name) for idempotency on restart
9	  - Logs every call with correlation IDs, latency, and token counts
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import json
15	import time
16	from typing import Any, Type, TypeVar
17	
18	import openai
19	from pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError
20	
21	from hiive_monitor import logging as log_module
22	from hiive_monitor.config import get_settings
23	
24	T = TypeVar("T", bound=BaseModel)
25	
26	_CALL_CACHE: dict[tuple[str, str, str], BaseModel] = {}
27	_SCHEMA_CACHE: dict[type[BaseModel], dict[str, Any]] = {}
28	
29	_MAX_RETRIES = 3
30	_RETRY_BASE_DELAY = 1.0
31	
32	
33	def _get_client() -> openai.OpenAI:
34	    settings = get_settings()
35	    return openai.OpenAI(
36	        api_key=settings.openrouter_api_key,
37	        base_url=settings.openrouter_base_url,
38	    )

41	def call_structured(
42	    *,
43	    prompt: str,
44	    output_model: Type[T],
45	    model: str,
46	    tick_id: str,
47	    deal_id: str,
48	    call_name: str,
49	    system: str = "",
50	    timeout: float = 30.0,
51	) -> T | None:
52	    """
53	    Make a schema-validated LLM call and return a typed Pydantic instance.
54	
55	    Returns None only if both the primary call and the corrective reprompt
56	    both fail validation. Caller is responsible for persisting an error observation.
57	    """
58	    cache_key = (tick_id, deal_id, call_name)
59	    if cache_key in _CALL_CACHE:
60	        return _CALL_CACHE[cache_key]  # type: ignore[return-value]
61	
62	    logger = log_module.get_logger()
63	    tool_name = call_name.replace(".", "_")
64	    tool_schema = _SCHEMA_CACHE.get(output_model)
65	    if tool_schema is None:
66	        tool_schema = _inline_refs(output_model.model_json_schema())
67	        _SCHEMA_CACHE[output_model] = tool_schema
68	
69	    tools = [
70	        {
71	            "type": "function",
72	            "function": {
73	                "name": tool_name,
74	                "description": f"Output schema for {call_name}",
75	                "parameters": tool_schema,
76	            },
77	        }
78	    ]
79	
80	    messages: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
81	    if system:
82	        messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system})
83	    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": prompt})
84	
85	    result = _call_with_retry(
86	        messages=messages,
87	        tools=tools,
88	        tool_name=tool_name,
89	        output_model=output_model,
90	        model=model,
91	        timeout=timeout,
92	        call_name=call_name,
93	        tick_id=tick_id,
94	        deal_id=deal_id,
95	        logger=logger,
96	    )
97	
98	    if result is not None:
99	        _CALL_CACHE[cache_key] = result
100	    return result

103	def _call_with_retry(
104	    *,
105	    messages: list[dict],
106	    tools: list[dict],
107	    tool_name: str,
108	    output_model: Type[T],
109	    model: str,
110	    timeout: float,
111	    call_name: str,
112	    tick_id: str,
113	    deal_id: str,
114	    logger: Any,
115	) -> T | None:
116	    client = _get_client()
117	    last_error: Exception | None = None
118	    parse_failure_msg: str | None = None
119	
120	    for attempt in range(1, _MAX_RETRIES + 1):
121	        t0 = time.monotonic()
122	        try:
123	            response = client.chat.completions.create(
124	                model=model,
125	                max_tokens=2048,
126	                tools=tools,
127	                tool_choice={"type": "function", "function": {"name": tool_name}},
128	                messages=messages,
129	                timeout=timeout,
130	            )
131	            latency_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)
132	
133	            choice = response.choices[0]
134	            tool_calls = choice.message.tool_calls
135	            if not tool_calls:
136	                raise ValueError(f"No tool_calls in response for {call_name}")
137	
138	            raw_json = tool_calls[0].function.arguments
139	            raw_input = json.loads(raw_json)
140	
141	            try:
142	                parsed = output_model.model_validate(raw_input)
143	                logger.info(
144	                    "llm.call.completed",
145	                    call_name=call_name,
146	                    model=model,
147	                    latency_ms=latency_ms,
148	                    input_tokens=response.usage.prompt_tokens if response.usage else 0,
149	                    output_tokens=response.usage.completion_tokens if response.usage else 0,
150	                    tick_id=tick_id,
151	                    deal_id=deal_id,
152	                    attempt=attempt,
153	                    parse_ok=True,
154	                )
155	                return parsed
156	
157	            except ValidationError as ve:
158	                latency_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)
159	                logger.warning(
160	                    "llm.call.parse_failure",
161	                    call_name=call_name,
162	                    model=model,
163	                    latency_ms=latency_ms,
164	                    tick_id=tick_id,
165	                    deal_id=deal_id,
166	                    attempt=attempt,
167	                    error=str(ve),
168	                )
169	                if parse_failure_msg is None:
170	                    parse_failure_msg = str(ve)
171	                    assistant_msg: dict[str, Any] = {
172	                        "role": "assistant",
173	                        "content": None,
174	                        "tool_calls": [
175	                            {
176	                                "id": tool_calls[0].id,
177	                                "type": "function",
178	                                "function": {
179	                                    "name": tool_name,
180	                                    "arguments": raw_json,
181	                                },
182	                            }
183	                        ],
184	                    }
185	                    tool_result_msg: dict[str, Any] = {
186	                        "role": "tool",
187	                        "tool_call_id": tool_calls[0].id,
188	                        "content": f"Validation error: {ve}",
189	                    }
190	                    corrective_msg: dict[str, Any] = {
191	                        "role": "user",
192	                        "content": (
193	                            f"Your previous response failed validation:\n{ve}\n\n"
194	                            "Please correct the output and respond again using the same tool schema."
195	                        ),
196	                    }
197	                    messages = messages + [assistant_msg, tool_result_msg, corrective_msg]
198	                    continue
199	                else:
200	                    logger.error(
201	                        "llm.call.parse_failure_final",
202	                        call_name=call_name,
203	                        model=model,
204	                        tick_id=tick_id,
205	                        deal_id=deal_id,
206	                        error=str(ve),
207	                    )
208	                    return None
209	
210	        except openai.BadRequestError as e:
211	            logger.error(
212	                "llm.call.bad_request",
213	                call_name=call_name,
214	                model=model,
215	                tick_id=tick_id,
216	                deal_id=deal_id,
217	                error=str(e),
218	            )
219	            return None
220	
221	        except (openai.APIError, openai.APIConnectionError, openai.RateLimitError) as e:
222	            latency_ms = int((time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000)
223	            last_error = e
224	            delay = _RETRY_BASE_DELAY * (2 ** (attempt - 1))
225	            logger.warning(
226	                "llm.call.transient_error",
227	                call_name=call_name,
228	                model=model,
229	                attempt=attempt,
230	                retry_in_s=delay,
231	                tick_id=tick_id,
232	                deal_id=deal_id,
233	                error=str(e),
234	            )
235	            if attempt < _MAX_RETRIES:
236	                time.sleep(delay)
237	
238	    logger.error(
239	        "llm.call.exhausted_retries",
240	        call_name=call_name,
241	        model=model,
242	        tick_id=tick_id,
243	        deal_id=deal_id,
244	        last_error=str(last_error),
245	    )
246	    return None

260	def _inline_refs(schema: dict) -> dict:
261	    """
262	    Flatten $defs/$ref references in a JSON schema so the tool API
263	    can parse it. Handles one level of nesting — sufficient for our models.
264	    """
265	    import copy
266	
267	    schema = copy.deepcopy(schema)
268	    defs = schema.pop("$defs", {})
269	    if not defs:
270	        return schema
271	
272	    def resolve(obj: Any) -> Any:
273	        if isinstance(obj, dict):
274	            if "$ref" in obj:
275	                ref_name = obj["$ref"].split("/")[-1]
276	                if ref_name in defs:
277	                    return resolve(copy.deepcopy(defs[ref_name]))
278	            return {k: resolve(v) for k, v in obj.items()}
279	        if isinstance(obj, list):
280	            return [resolve(item) for item in obj]
281	        return obj
282	
283	    return resolve(schema)

2	Deterministic counterparty non-responsiveness evaluator (FR-004, Dimension #5).
3	
4	Pure Python — no LLM call. Compares days_since_last_comm against the issuer's
5	typical_response_days baseline to produce a RiskSignal.
6	"""

9	
10	from hiive_monitor.models.risk import RiskDimension, RiskSignal
11	from hiive_monitor.models.snapshot import DealSnapshot
12	
13	
14	def evaluate_counterparty_responsiveness(
15	    snapshot: DealSnapshot,
16	    issuer_typical_response_days: int,
17	) -> RiskSignal:
18	    """
19	    Returns a triggered RiskSignal if days_since_last_comm exceeds the issuer
20	    typical_response_days threshold (×1.5 buffer applied).
21	    """
22	    if snapshot.days_since_last_comm is None:
23	        return RiskSignal(
24	            dimension=RiskDimension.COUNTERPARTY_NONRESPONSIVENESS,
25	            triggered=False,
26	            evidence="No communication history available to assess responsiveness.",
27	            confidence=0.3,
28	        )
29	
30	    threshold = max(issuer_typical_response_days, 3)  # floor at 3 days
31	    silence = snapshot.days_since_last_comm
32	    ratio = silence / threshold
33	
34	    if ratio >= 1.5:
35	        return RiskSignal(
36	            dimension=RiskDimension.COUNTERPARTY_NONRESPONSIVENESS,
37	            triggered=True,
38	            evidence=(
39	                f"{snapshot.issuer_name} typically responds within {threshold} days; "
40	                f"{snapshot.deal_id} has had no communication for {silence} days "
41	                f"({ratio:.1f}× threshold). Responsible party: {snapshot.responsible_party}."
42	            ),
43	            confidence=min(0.95, 0.5 + (ratio - 1.5) * 0.3),
44	        )
45	    else:
46	        return RiskSignal(
47	            dimension=RiskDimension.COUNTERPARTY_NONRESPONSIVENESS,
48	            triggered=False,
49	            evidence=(
50	                f"Communication gap of {silence} days is within {threshold}-day "
51	                f"response window for {snapshot.issuer_name} ({ratio:.1f}× threshold)."
52	            ),
53	            confidence=0.8,
54	        )

1	"""Post-parse validator ensuring LLM risk-signal evidence references the specific deal."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import re
6	
7	from hiive_monitor.models.risk import RiskSignal
8	from hiive_monitor.models.snapshot import DealSnapshot
9	
10	_BLOCKER_FRAGMENT_LEN = 30
11	_COUNTERPARTY_ROLES = frozenset({"buyer", "seller", "issuer", "assignee", "counterparty"})
12	_RE_DAY_COUNT = re.compile(r"\b\d+\s*day")
13	_RE_DATE = re.compile(r"\b\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\b")
14	
15	
16	def assert_evidence_references_deal(signal: RiskSignal, snapshot: DealSnapshot) -> bool:
17	    """Return True if evidence contains at least one deal-specific token."""
18	    ev = signal.evidence.lower()
19	
20	    if snapshot.issuer_name.lower() in ev:
21	        return True
22	
23	    if snapshot.stage.value.lower() in ev:
24	        return True
25	
26	    if _RE_DAY_COUNT.search(ev):
27	        return True
28	
29	    if _RE_DATE.search(ev):
30	        return True
31	
32	    if any(role in ev for role in _COUNTERPARTY_ROLES):
33	        return True
34	
35	    for blocker in snapshot.blockers:
36	        fragment = blocker.description[:_BLOCKER_FRAGMENT_LEN].lower()
37	        if fragment and fragment in ev:
38	            return True
39	
40	    return False

6	(communication history, prior observations, issuer history) would materially change

17	context to confidently score this deal's risk severity.
18	
19	You have three enrichment tools available:
20	  - fetch_communication_content: returns last 10 comm events (body text). Use when a silence \
21	    signal triggered and you want to know WHY (legal hold? holiday? explicit delay?).
22	  - fetch_prior_observations: returns last 5 agent observations. Use when you see compounding \
23	    signals and want to know if this is a new pattern or recurring.
24	  - fetch_issuer_history: returns prior settled/broken deals for this issuer. Use when \
25	    unusual_characteristics triggered and issuer-level breakage history would be material.
26	
27	Be conservative: if the risk signals are clear-cut (e.g., ROFR in 1 day, no ambiguity), \
28	call sufficient=True immediately. Only request enrichment when the additional context \
29	would actually change the severity decision.
30	
31	Max 2 enrichment rounds total per deal per tick. Keep rationale under 300 characters. Reply only via the required tool."""

47	        if enrichment_context

61	{context_text}
62

21	def compose_daily_brief(tick_id: str) -> DailyBrief | None:

23	    settings = get_settings()

33	        except (ValueError, TypeError):

1	# Hiive Deal Pipeline Monitoring Agent
2	
3	An agentic monitoring system for Hiive's Transaction Services team. Two LangGraph agents — a
4	**Pipeline Monitor** that screens every live deal on each tick and a **Deal Investigator** that
5	observes, evaluates six risk dimensions, and drafts interventions — help Transaction Services
6	analysts answer the question: *"Tell me what I should do today, in priority order, with the draft
7	already written, and show me why you're recommending it."*

9	## Quickstart

1	# Reflection
2	
3	The most interesting design choice was the enrichment loop inside the Deal Investigator: rather than running all six risk dimensions and immediately scoring severity, the agent's `assess_sufficiency` node can pause, call a targeted tool (comm history, prior observations, issuer history), and loop back — up to two rounds before being forced to a verdict. This is what makes it genuinely agentic rather than a dressed-up pipeline: the number of steps, the tools called, and the context assembled all vary per deal and per tick based on LLM reasoning. The hardest part to get right was the LangGraph compiled-graph closure semantics — the graph captures function bindings at compile time, which meant integration tests had to call node functions directly rather than relying on post-compile monkeypatching. The second-hardest was the clock abstraction: threading a single simulated-time source through every timestamp read in the system (LangGraph state, DAO queries, scheduler ticks, seed data generation) without any `datetime.now()` leaking through — the static analysis test that scans source files for bare `datetime.now()` calls exists precisely because this kind of leak is invisible until a time-dependent test flakes.
4

1	# Impeccable Design Context
2	
3	## Design Context
4	
5	### Users
6	Transaction Services analysts at Hiive — a FINRA-member pre-IPO secondary stock marketplace. Power users processing 30–60 live deals per day on a dual-monitor desk setup. They scan dense information rapidly and need speed and scannability over visual polish. They are not designers; they are expert operators. The interface must feel like a tool built by people who understand financial operations.
7	
8	### Brand Personality
9	**Three words:** Precise, calm, trustworthy.
10	
11	No personality. No playfulness. The interface must recede so deals take center stage. The green brand color is a small mark of identity — it should not dominate.
12	
13	### Aesthetic Direction
14	**Concept:** "The Quiet Architect" — dense, calm, trustworthy, precise. Linear.app and Height.app are the reference targets. This is a compliance-adjacent ops tool, not consumer SaaS.
15	
16	**Theme:** Both light and dark mode (user-toggleable). Default to the light mode defined in the design system.
17	
18	**Reference apps:** Linear.app, Height.app — dense, minimal, professional.
19	
20	**Anti-references:**
21	- Generic AI dashboards with colorful stat cards and hero numbers
22	- Purple or blue gradient backgrounds of any kind
23	- Glassmorphism, drop shadows, rounded pill cards
24	- Cards nested inside cards
25	- Inter as a *display* font (use it for UI body only)
26	- Decorative icons everywhere
27	- Emoji anywhere in the product UI
28	
29	### Color Palette
30	
31	**No-Shadow Depth Model** — depth through tonal layering only, never drop shadows:
32	- `surface`: `#f8f9fa` — canvas / page background
33	- `surface-container-low`: `#f3f4f5` — large organizational sections
34	- `surface-container-lowest`: `#ffffff` — cards, inputs (creates "lifted" effect)
35	
36	**Brand colors:**
37	- **Primary (Hiive Green):** `#1B4332` — active nav, primary buttons, authority actions, logo
38	- **Secondary:** `#2D6A4F` — accents, hover states, progress indicators
39	- **Neutral text:** `#414844` (on-surface-variant)
40	- **Ghost border:** `outline-variant` `#c1c8c2` at 50% opacity rendered as 0.5px stroke
41	
42	**Semantic overrides (severity):**
43	- ESCALATE: text `#ba1a1a` / bg `#ffdad6` / 0.5px border at 10% `#ba1a1a`
44	- ACT: text `#1B4332` / bg `#c1ecd4` / 0.5px border at 10% `#1B4332`
45	- WATCH: text `#401b1b` / bg `#ffdad8` / 0.5px border at 10% `#401b1b`
46	- INFORMATIONAL: text `#414844` / bg `#edeeef` / 0.5px border at 10% `#414844`
47	
48	Use OKLCH in CSS for all palette definitions. Tint all neutrals toward `#1B4332` hue (chroma 0.005–0.01).
49	
50	### Typography

15	│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
16	│  │  APScheduler  (in-process, two modes)                   │   │
17	│  │  ┌──────────┐  real_time: IntervalTrigger(60s)          │   │
18	│  │  │ run_tick │  simulated: manual advance via /sim/advance│   │
19	│  │  └────┬─────┘                                           │   │
20	│  └───────│─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
21	│          │                                                       │
22	│  ┌───────▼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
23	│  │  Pipeline Monitor  (LangGraph StateGraph)                │   │
24	│  │  load_live_deals → screen_with_haiku → apply_suppression │   │
25	│  │  → select_investigation_queue → fan_out_investigators    │   │
26	│  │  → close_tick                                            │   │
27	│  │                                                          │   │
28	│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │   │
29	│  │  │  Deal Investigator × up to 5  (LangGraph sub-graph│   │   │
30	│  │  │  observe → evaluate_risks → assess_sufficiency   │   │   │
31	│  │  │  ↕ agentic loop (max 2 rounds)                   │   │   │
32	│  │  │  enrich_context → assess_sufficiency             │   │   │
33	│  │  │  → score_severity → [draft_intervention]         │   │   │
34	│  │  │  → emit_observation                              │   │   │
35	│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │   │
36	│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
37	│                                                                 │
38	│  ┌──────────────────┐   ┌──────────────────────────────────┐   │
39	│  │  domain.db       │   │  agent_checkpoints.db            │   │
40	│  │  (SQLite)        │   │  (LangGraph SqliteSaver)         │   │
41	│  │  deals           │   │  Per (tick_id, deal_id) thread   │   │
42	│  │  events          │   └──────────────────────────────────┘   │
43	│  │  agent_observations│                                        │
44	│  │  interventions   │                                          │
45	│  │  issuers         │                                          │
46	│  │  parties         │                                          │
47	│  │  ticks           │                                          │
48	│  └──────────────────┘                                          │
49	│                                                                 │
50	│  Claude Haiku ← screening (cheap, per-deal)                    │
51	│  Claude Sonnet ← risk evaluation, severity, intervention draft │
52	└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
53	```
54	
55	## Key Design Decisions
56	
57	**Clock abstraction**: Every timestamp read goes through `hiive_monitor.clock.now()`. Two modes selectable by `CLOCK_MODE` env var: `real_time` (wall clock) and `simulated` (injected, advanceable). The eval harness and `make demo` both use simulated mode. No `datetime.now()` calls anywhere in application code.
58	
59	**Agentic loop**: The Deal Investigator's N3→N4→N3 loop (assess_sufficiency → enrich_context → assess_sufficiency) is bounded at 2 rounds to prevent runaway LLM costs. The LLM decides which enrichment tool to call (communication history, prior observations, or issuer history). If the max rounds are reached, the graph forces "sufficient" and proceeds to severity scoring.
60	
61	**Idempotency**: Two layers. (1) The `ticks` table has UNIQUE on `tick_id` — `start_tick` uses `INSERT OR IGNORE`. If a tick crashes and restarts with the same tick_id, it's a no-op. (2) The `agent_observations` table has UNIQUE on `(tick_id, deal_id)` — double-invocation of the investigator for the same deal in the same tick is silently discarded.
62	
63	**Human-in-the-loop**: The agent never sends external communications. All interventions are drafts in `status='pending'`. Approve and edit both use atomic DB transactions that simultaneously update the intervention status and insert a `comm_sent_agent_recommended` event — this is the only path to marking a communication as sent.
64	
65	**Suppression**: Deals that received an agent-recommended communication within the last 3 ticks have their attention score multiplied by 0.2. This prevents re-nudging a party that was already contacted this tick window.
66	
67	## Two-Tier LLM Strategy
68	
69	| Model | Used for | Cost profile |
70	|-------|----------|-------------|
71	| Claude Haiku | Per-deal attention scoring (screen_with_haiku) | Low — one call per live deal per tick |
72	| Claude Sonnet | Risk evaluation (5 calls), severity scoring, intervention drafting, enrichment sufficiency | Medium — only for top-5 deals per tick |
73	
74	## Evaluation Architecture
75	
76	```
77	eval/fixtures/*.yaml  →  runner.py
78	  ├── _seed_from_scenario()  builds isolated in-memory DB
79	  ├── SimulatedClock set to scenario.setup.now
80	  ├── run_tick() invokes full Monitor + Investigator
81	  └── evaluate_assertions() checks observations + interventions
82	        → scorecard_{timestamp}.md
83	```
84	
85	17 scenarios across 3 categories:
86	- **detection** (10): verifies the agent flags the right risks
87	- **prioritization** (4): verifies severity ordering
88	- **intervention_quality** (3): verifies draft content against domain-accuracy requirements
89
